title: Rebuild of Evanatomy Mass Displacement and Wacky Diracy
display_title: Rebuild of Evanatomy Mass Displacement and Wacky Diracy
wikitext:
[]: Rebuild of Evanatomy: Mass Displacement and Wacky Diracy             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 22, 2010 10:09 pm This is a post I've been meaning to make since May. But I didn't because I actually have, you know, A LIFE. No, just messing around. I don't. Evangelion anatomy in NME is obviously pretty different from in NGE. Unit 02 shows us just how different with Beast Mode. The "armor" isn't metal this time around; it's something fleshy and malleable. And this time, it's not even something that the Evas "wear". It's their skin... or, more accurately, an exoskeleton. There's no skull to hold the brains in. Just the red notarmor. The teeth aren't stuck into a jawbone. Just the notarmor. None of the Evangelion anatomy that applied in NGE applies to the new movies. From here I'm going to move onto mass displacement. The Sixth Angel, obviously, was capable of changing size and shape in a manner that seemed to show that it existed in more than three dimensions. The Tenth Angel is also capable of mass displacement. The giant inner mouth that came out and chomped Rei is one example. Another example is the way the Angel used its "arms" to shoot out ballistically at Unit 02. There should be no doubt that the Tenth Angel is capable of changing mass at will, indicating it may also be a supertridimensional entity. Now, onto Unit 01. At the end of the film, Shinji's entry plug descends into "The Great Beyond Depth", further than the Terminal Depth. Basically, Shinji shouldn't really be able to go this far into the Eva. This sort of space just doesn't exist inside an 80 meter monster. But Shinji enters this depth without regard for what should or shouldn't be possible, then somehow enters an equivalent space inside the Tenth Angel by crawling through some sort of psychedelic tunnel. Obviously, this space we're seeing isn't actually a physical space that exists in our perceptible three dimensions. Unit 01's torso is over myah and the Tenth Angel's core is over myah. Yet Shinji crawls through and then stuff happens and yeah. The closest equivalent we've seen to this sort of thing in NGE is the Sea of Dirac. Basically what I'm trying to say is that Angels and Evangelions are supertridimensional entities which are capable of interacting with each other in terms of these higher dimensions. This allows Shinji to enter Unit 01 so far that he should come out the other side but keep going in until he's inside the Tenth Angel. This also explains how the restrictors/limiters/hair curlers inside Unit 02 are able to fit despite apparently being too big, if you feel the need to explain that. Which Reichu does. There, Reichu, I explained it. Can Beast Mode be cool now?

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Thu Jul 22, 2010 10:17 pm Allow me to golf-clap. . . . Well, that was fun. Fascinating read, as always. I have nothing of any importance to add, but I'll be eagerly awaiting what anyone else has to say.

[]: Re: Rebuild of Evanatomy: Mass Displacement and Wacky Diracy             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Thu Jul 22, 2010 10:23 pm NAveryW wrote: The Sixth Angel, obviously, was capable of changing size and shape in a manner that seemed to show that it existed in more than three dimensions.

[]: Re: Rebuild of Evanatomy: Mass Displacement and Wacky Diracy             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 22, 2010 10:42 pm Killer Bee wrote: wat.

[]:             Quote Post by Paintballer » Fri Jul 23, 2010 12:06 am Take away the face and some of the jaw and you have the eva series looking head

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Fri Jul 23, 2010 12:20 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Fri Jul 23, 2010 10:46 am NAW wrote: But Shinji enters this depth without regard for what should or shouldn't be possible, then somehow enters an equivalent space inside the Tenth Angel by crawling through some sort of psychedelic tunnel. Obviously, this space we're seeing isn't actually a physical space that exists in our perceptible three dimensions.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Jul 23, 2010 11:08 am carla wrote: this is assuming that Shinji crawling through the angel's core to get to Rei was... actually happening, right? (i always just took it as a representation of Shinji's feelings-- and maybe Rei's feelings, as well-- on a metaphysical "plane". never actually thought to measure the actual distance. hmm).

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Jul 23, 2010 11:30 am One thing that indicates Shinji physically dived inside Zeruel to rescue Rei: The SDAT Player. Rei takes it into Eva-00, and it's eaten by Zer. She's still holding it when she's pulled out by Shinji. There really is something bizarre going on here.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Fri Jul 23, 2010 11:35 am ...

[]: Re: Rebuild of Evanatomy: Mass Displacement and Wacky Diracy             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Fri Jul 23, 2010 12:26 pm NAveryW wrote: Yes, superstring theory postulates dozens more dimensions and all that, but I'm talking about dimensions that aren't "curled up inside atoms", as one documentary put it in a way that would hopefully make a lot more sense if explained properly.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jul 23, 2010 12:33 pm carla wrote: (i always just took it as a representation of Shinji's feelings-- and maybe Rei's feelings, as well-- on a metaphysical "plane". never actually thought to measure the actual distance. hmm).

[]: Take a shot of vodka every time I say "Dirac Sea."             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Fri Jul 23, 2010 12:59 pm NAveryW wrote: we have NGE's precedent of an Angel holding higher physical dimensions within itself.

[]: I'm a teetotaler.             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jul 23, 2010 1:26 pm Killer Bee wrote: But it doesn't. You're using an outdated concept (the Dirac Sea) to explain a brand new enigma (Unit-01 core-diving). You say the actual Dirac Sea doesn't matter, but it's the only instance of something happening like that in NGE. If it's been written out of the canon, so to speak, then its use as a precedent is worthless.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinies » Fri Jul 23, 2010 1:56 pm I'm kinda new here but not to the series, and I don't think I'm qualified to enter a discussion yet but something has been bothering me in those 2 last post of you Killer Bee, and it's the fact that the one writing off the Dirac Sea thing is you. I'm sure you know a lot about physics and math, so it would be sensless for me to say that even though is not mentioned in the rebuild (I don't know what NME stands for) it IS there. In space actually. And the Dirac Sea does not depend of an angel to exist. Other than that I'll say, it's not impossible to be physically inside the angel core while in the Eva, remember that by this point Eva 01 has godlike abilities and that by definition means he can do almost anything, even if it breaks or bends our current understanding of physics.

[]: Re: I'm a teetotaler.             Quote Post by Velefor » Fri Jul 23, 2010 2:21 pm I wonder did I and the topic starter watched the same movie. All the assumptions made in the first post seem totally absurd to me. NAveryW wrote: There's no skull to hold the brains in. Just the red notarmor.

[]: Re: I'm a teetotaler.             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jul 23, 2010 2:41 pm Velefor wrote: Was there ever a solid skull?

[]: Re: I'm a teetotaler.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Fri Jul 23, 2010 4:52 pm Velefor wrote: Their anatomies look exactly the same to me. I yet haven't seen any convincing proofs of any major deference.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jul 23, 2010 5:09 pm I figured that similar to Instrumentality/hell train stuff/whatever else in NGE, Operation Rescue Ayanami in 2.22 is more or less a metaphysical depiction (Shinji going past the plug depth/the core of Eva-01 to enter Zeruel) that results from a physical action (Rei and Eva-00 being eaten by Zer, Shinji "going berserk" with his frustration of Eva-01 shutting down/wanting to defeat the 10th Angel). By the way, Mari "goes berserk" earlier and she's still in one piece, although because her eye color is different than Shinji's (green and red are opposites apparently), her berserker probably has a different reason behind it. Anyway, Ritsuko babbled about Shinji "not being able to return to human form" (though she says that a lot in the film), so I guess Shinji's leaving the entry plug (we see him rip off part of the seat before he starts crawling) is when his physical form is lost. Basically there is some spiritual/metaphysical reaction going on considering once Rei is saved, we cut from this: to this: Shinji and Rei seem to be fading away. Whether or not they're losing their physical forms (tanging, just depicted differently for stylish reasons? Wouldn't be the first time.) or already lost their bodies, Rei and Shinji seem to be disappearing/dissolving into Eva-01 at least. Come to think of it, the script would possibly help make sense of what's going on in location to where (eg: "Within the Angel" versus "Within/outside of Eva-01"). Anybody have it on hand? Another thought occurs, though Kuma pointed this out. What Misato's cross is (as Shinji's link to reality) during EoE is what the SDAT is to Rei and Shinji in 2.22. Both objects are physically present despite the metaphysical wackiness going on.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jul 23, 2010 5:13 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Another thought occurs, though Kuma pointed this out. What Misato's cross is (as Shinji's link to reality) during EoE is what the SDAT is to Rei and Shinji in 2.22. Both objects are physically present despite the metaphysical wackiness going on.
